is a [constituency](/wiki/List_of_UK_Parliament_constituencies "List of UK Parliament constituencies") of the [House of Commons](/wiki/House_of_Commons_of_the_United_Kingdom "House of Commons of the United Kingdom") in the [UK Parliament](/wiki/Parliament_of_the_United_Kingdom "Parliament of the United Kingdom"). The constituency was created as a result of the [2023 Periodic Review of Westminster constituencies](/wiki/2023_Periodic_Review_of_Westminster_constituencies "2023 Periodic Review of Westminster constituencies") and was first contested at the [2024 general election](/wiki/2024_United_Kingdom_general_election "2024 United Kingdom general election").[[2]](#cite_note-2) It is represented by [Neil Shastri-Hurst](/wiki/Neil_Shastri-Hurst "Neil Shastri-Hurst") of the [Conservative Party](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK) "Conservative Party (UK)").

["The 2023 Review of Parliamentary Constituency Boundaries in England – Volume two: Constituency names, designations and composition – West Midlands"](https://boundarycommissionforengland.independent.gov.uk/2023-review/the-2023-review-of-parliamentary-constituency-boundaries-in-england-volume-two-constituency-names-designations-and-composition/the-2023-review-of-parliamentary-constituency-boundaries-in-england-volume-two-constituency-names-designations-and-composition-west-midlands/#lg_solihull-west-and-shirley-bc-70537). From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

UK Parliament constituency (since 2024)

| Solihull West and Shirley | |

| Seats | One |
| Created from | * [Solihull](/wiki/Solihull_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Solihull (UK Parliament constituency)") (Majority) * [Meriden](/wiki/Meriden_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Meriden (UK Parliament constituency)") (Minority) |

**Solihull West and Shirley** is a [constituency](/wiki/List_of_UK_Parliament_constituencies "List of UK Parliament constituencies") of the [House of Commons](/wiki/House_of_Commons_of_the_United_Kingdom "House of Commons of the United Kingdom") in the [UK Parliament](/wiki/Parliament_of_the_United_Kingdom "Parliament of the United Kingdom"). The constituency was created as a result of the [2023 Periodic Review of Westminster constituencies](/wiki/2023_Periodic_Review_of_Westminster_constituencies "2023 Periodic Review of Westminster constituencies") and was first contested at the [2024 general election](/wiki/2024_United_Kingdom_general_election "2024 United Kingdom general election").[[2]](#cite_note-2) It is represented by [Neil Shastri-Hurst](/wiki/Neil_Shastri-Hurst "Neil Shastri-Hurst") of the [Conservative Party](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK) "Conservative Party (UK)").

The constituency name refers to the suburban town of [Shirley](/wiki/Shirley,_West_Midlands "Shirley, West Midlands") and the western areas of the [Metropolitan Borough of Solihull](/wiki/Metropolitan_Borough_of_Solihull "Metropolitan Borough of Solihull").[[3]](#cite_note-3) This is part of a boundary review that resulted in the borough being split between three Westminster constituencies.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Boundaries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Solihull_West_and_Shirley&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Boundaries")]

The constituency is composed of the following (as they existed on 1 December 2020):

* The Metropolitan Borough of Solihull wards of: Blythe; Lyndon; Olton; St. Alphege; Shirley East; Shirley South; Shirley West.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

It contains the following areas:[[6]](#cite_note-6)

* Blythe ward (17.9% of this constituency) from the previous [Meriden constituency](/wiki/Meriden_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Meriden (UK Parliament constituency)") (abolished)
* The majority (82.1%) of the previous [Solihull constituency](/wiki/Solihull_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Solihull (UK Parliament constituency)") (abolished)
